step_by_step_thinking: Staphylococcus albus, Streptococcus faecalis, Salmonella typhi, and Pseudomonas aeruginosa all have different characteristics regarding their ability to cause infective endocarditis. However, among them, \boxed{C} is less likely to cause infective endocarditis because it primarily causes gastrointestinal symptoms rather than bloodstream infections typically seen in endocarditis cases. Additionally, Salmonella typhi usually affects the intestines and rarely involves the heart valves directly unless there's a disseminated infection, making it even more unlikely compared to others listed here. Therefore, \boxed{C}.
